steps.title: Add the radian measures
steps.content:
Angle $P$ is $\frac{5\pi}{12}$ radians greater than angle $M$, so add the two radian measures:
$$P=\frac{2\pi}{3}+\frac{5\pi}{12}$$
$$P=\frac{8\pi}{12}+\frac{5\pi}{12}$$
$$P=\frac{13\pi}{12}$$

steps.title: Convert radians to degrees
steps.content:
Convert radians to degrees by multiplying by $\frac{180^\circ}{\pi}$. The factors of $\pi$ cancel, so the degree measure is:
$$\frac{13\pi}{12}\cdot \frac{180^\circ}{\pi}=195^\circ$$The answer is C.
